# Phase 2 Design Annexes — verbatim specs behind the PRD

*Generated by the Phase 2 design round (2026-07-06). The PRD (orchestrator-v2-phase2-prd.md) is scope/priority; these annexes are the letter of the spec.*

---

# Annex A — Phone Channel

## Facts (researched July 2026)

**Twilio SMS (A2P 10DLC, US solo dev):**
- Sole-proprietor registration (no EIN needed): ~$4.50 one-time brand + $15 one-time campaign vetting + ~$2/mo campaign fee; number ~$1.15/mo. Brand approval is minutes–days, but **campaign reviews currently run 10–15 days** — realistic time-to-first-compliant-message is **2–3 weeks**. ([Twilio A2P pricing](https://help.twilio.com/articles/1260803965530), [Sole Proprietor overview](https://www.twilio.com/docs/messaging/compliance/a2p-10dlc/direct-sole-proprietor-registration-overview), [10DLC 2026 guide](https://tuco.ai/a2p-10dlc))
- Per message: ~$0.0083 base + $0.003–0.005 carrier fee ≈ **$0.012/segment** each way. Sole-prop caps: 1 number, 1 MPS, ~3,000 msgs/day US (1,000 to T-Mobile) — volume is a non-issue for dgorch. ([Twilio US SMS pricing](https://www.twilio.com/en-us/sms/pricing/us), [T-Mobile limits](https://help.twilio.com/articles/1260804800549))
- Toll-free is not faster anymore: verification takes ~3–5 business days to 2 weeks, and **since Feb 17, 2026 a Business Registration Number (EIN) is mandatory** for new toll-free verifications. ([Twilio TFV policy](https://help.twilio.com/articles/38266268244507), [TFV help](https://help.twilio.com/articles/5377174717595))
- Two-way SMS requires a **public webhook URL** (ngrok tunnel on a laptop) and a TwiML response. ([Twilio webhooks](https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/webhooks/getting-started-twilio-webhooks))

**Telegram Bot API:**
- Free, ~5-minute setup via @BotFather. `getUpdates` long-polling (25–30s timeout) works with **no public URL** — perfect for a laptop process; webhook and polling are mutually exclusive. ([Bot API](https://core.telegram.org/bots/api), [polling vs webhook](https://gramio.dev/updates/webhook))
- Inline keyboard buttons with `callback_data` give **one-tap answers**; must call `answerCallbackQuery`. 4,096-char message limit; 30 msg/s global, ~20 msg/min per chat — orders of magnitude above dgorch's needs. ([Bots FAQ](https://core.telegram.org/bots/faq), [rate limits](https://gramio.dev/rate-limits))

**Third option:** [ntfy.sh](https://ntfy.sh/) — one curl, free (~250 msgs/day), push to phone, **notification-only** (no replies). Pushover similar, $4.99 one-time. Fine fallbacks, can't answer questions.

## Recommendation

**Telegram, day one.** SMS was the ideal, but honestly: A2P sole-prop means 2–3 weeks of registration limbo, ~$3/mo + per-message fees, and two-way replies force a public URL/ngrok tunnel onto a laptop process — three moving parts Telegram simply doesn't have. Telegram is free, live in 5 minutes, long-polls from the laptop with zero exposure, and inline buttons beat parsing "1"/"A" from SMS. Ship the `Notifier` interface so a Twilio adapter can be added if/when dgorch moves to the GCP VM (which has a public URL). Tradeoff: it's an app notification, not a raw text — acceptable.

## Design

**Notifier interface** (new `src/notifier.ts`; Conductor calls it fire-and-forget):

```ts
export type NotifyEvent =
  | { type: "plan_ready"; runId: string; taskCount: number; summary: string }
  | { type: "human_review"; task: Task }
  | { type: "question"; questionId: string; prompt: string; options: string[]; recommended: string }
  | { type: "run_complete"; runId: string; stats: { done: number; total: number; costTokens: number } }
  | { type: "run_stuck"; runId: string; reason: string }
  | { type: "cost_threshold"; runId: string; spent: number; cap: number };

export interface Notifier {
  notify(event: NotifyEvent): Promise<void>;        // must NEVER throw into the conductor
  replies?(): AsyncIterable<InboundReply>;           // absent on notify-only channels (ntfy)
}
export interface InboundReply {
  externalId: string;          // Telegram update_id / Twilio MessageSid — idempotency key
  questionId: string | null;   // from callback_data "q:<id>:<letter>", else null (free text)
  text: string;
}
```

**Two-way flow:** questions live in a SQLite `questions` table (`id, task_id, prompt, options_json, recommended, status open|answered, answer, answered_via`). Buttons carry `q:<id>:<letter>`, so taps map exactly. Free-text "1"/"A"/"yes" maps to the **single** open question; if several are open, the bot re-prompts with the list ("reply `Q7 A`"). Idempotency: `externalId` is unique-indexed in `inbound_replies`; duplicates are no-ops. **First-wins:** answering marks the question row answered and applies the verdict via the existing `store.transition`, which is guarded by current task status (same guard `applyVerdicts` in `src/verdicts.ts` relies on) — if Linear already resolved it, the phone answer is a logged no-op and the bot replies "already resolved via Linear (approve)". Human-review replies: `ok 12` → approve; `fix 12 <note>` → changes + durable lesson, identical to the Linear path.

**Message format** (one line, ≤300 chars, prefix `[dgorch <project>]`):
- Question: `[dgorch shinesty] Q7: Stripe or Paddle for billing? A) Stripe B) Paddle — rec A (native invoicing). Tap or reply A/B.` (+ buttons `A) Stripe | B) Paddle | Go with rec`)
- Review: `[dgorch shinesty] Review "Checkout flow" (12/18). Approve in Linear, or reply: ok 12 / fix 12 <note>`
- Stuck: `[dgorch shinesty] STUCK: merge conflict on task 9 after 3 attempts. See Linear.`

**Noise control:** immediate for `question`, `run_stuck`, `cost_threshold`, `run_complete`; `human_review` batched into a 30-min digest (immediate when it's the last outstanding task). Quiet hours (default 22:00–08:00): only `run_stuck` breaks through; the rest arrive as one morning digest. Hard cap 10 msgs/hour; overflow collapses to "…and N more — see Linear."

**Failure behavior:** every `notify()` is try/catch + 5s timeout; 3 consecutive failures → degraded mode with backoff (max 15 min). Undelivered notifications are **not** durably queued — Linear holds all state; the channel is a mirror, never load-bearing. The replies loop persists its `getUpdates` offset in SQLite, so crash/resume never double-processes a tap.

## Config

- **Telegram (day one):** `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` (from @BotFather). Chat ID auto-captured: user sends `/start` once; bot stores `chat_id` in SQLite. That's the entire setup.
- **Twilio (later, VM only):** `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, `TWILIO_FROM`, `TWILIO_TO`, `WEBHOOK_BASE_URL` — plus completed sole-prop A2P registration.
- **Shared:** `NOTIFY_CHANNEL=telegram|none`, `NOTIFY_QUIET_HOURS=22-08`, `NOTIFY_MAX_PER_HOUR=10`.

## Risks

- Telegram has no SLA; OS may mute the app. Mitigation: critical events also mark the Linear issue Urgent; ntfy.sh is a one-line secondary ping if ever needed.
- Two dgorch processes long-polling one token → 409 conflicts. Enforce a single-instance lock in SQLite.
- Free-text ambiguity with multiple open questions — mitigated by buttons + `Q<id> <letter>` fallback.
- If SMS is revisited: budget 2–3 weeks A2P lead time and a public URL; don't block Phase 2 on it.

---

# Annex B — Personas & Review Panel

## Persona model

A persona is one markdown file, no new infra:

```
personas/
  workers/    general.md  frontend.md  backend.md  database.md  docs.md
  reviewers/  correctness.md  security.md  design-fidelity.md  test-quality.md
```

Format: YAML frontmatter + body.

```yaml
---
name: security
stage: reviewer          # worker | reviewer
model: claude-sonnet-4-5 # optional override; omit = run default
maxTurns: 15             # optional effort cap passed to claude -p
---
<mission / hunt list / ignore list / output contract>
```

Loading: a `loadPersona(name, repoPath)` helper reads the file at call time and prepends the body to the existing prompt builders (`buildWorkerPrompt` in `src/agent.ts`, `buildReviewPrompt` in `src/reviewer.ts`). The file's content hash is journaled with the review event so resume/audit knows exactly which persona text ran. No DB tables, no watchers — edit the file, next task uses it.

## Worker routing

The planner tags, the conductor routes. Add one enum to `PlannedTask` in `src/planner.ts`:

```
kind: "frontend" | "backend" | "database" | "docs" | "general"  (default "general")
```

One line in the planner prompt: "Tag each task with the single best `kind`." Conductor resolves `personas/workers/<kind>.md`; missing file → `general.md` (today's prompt, verbatim). Routing is prompt content only — no worker pools, no scheduling change, mis-tagged tasks still run fine because every persona is a competent coder with a different emphasis.

## Review panel

`ReviewPanel implements Reviewer` (drop-in for `CliReviewer` at `src/conductor.ts:28`). It runs **correctness first, short-circuits on reject** (the fix re-triggers the whole panel, so later reviews of doomed code are wasted spend), then runs triggered specialists in parallel.

| Reviewer | Trigger | Runs on ~% of tasks |
|---|---|---|
| correctness | every task | 100% |
| security | code-side heuristic on the diff (below) | ~40–60% |
| design-fidelity | `task.userVisible === true`; prompt includes design-ref paths from run config | ~25% |
| test-quality | diff adds >100 source LOC with no test-file changes, OR acceptance criteria mention tests | ~30% |
| performance | **off by default** — gates + correctness catch the obvious cases; enable per-project via override | 0% |

**Security trigger (heuristic, not every task):** fire when any of — (a) diff touches files matching `auth|login|session|middleware|api/|routes|db|migration|*.env*|config`; (b) dependency manifests change (`package.json`, lockfiles, `requirements*.txt`); (c) the diff text greps for `fetch|axios|exec|eval|spawn|process.env|SECRET|API_KEY|innerHTML|dangerouslySetInnerHTML|sql`; (d) `task.kind` is backend/database. This is deterministic code (a function over `git diff`), free to evaluate, and deliberately broad — it over-fires on safe changes rather than under-firing on risky ones. Every-task security review costs ~2× more for near-zero yield on docs/CSS tasks; the heuristic keeps the safety while human review still backstops all userVisible work.

## Aggregation & bounds

- **Output contract (all reviewers):** findings as `SEVERITY(critical|high|medium|low): <one line>`, final line `VERDICT: APPROVE` or `VERDICT: REJECT: <reason>`. Existing `parseReviewVerdict` fail-safe stays per-reviewer: unparseable = reject.
- **Combine:** any reviewer REJECT ⇒ task rejected. Reviewers are instructed to reject only on critical/high findings; medium/low findings are collected and posted as a Linear comment (advice, not a loop).
- **Routing back:** all rejecting reviewers' reasons concatenate, prefixed by reviewer name (`[security] ...; [correctness] ...`), into the existing `fail()` path — the retry worker prompt gets them via the lessons mechanism already in `src/conductor.ts:246`.
- **Anti-loop:** new `reviewRounds` counter per task, **max 2** bot-review round-trips. Round 3 needed ⇒ transition to `blocked` with the findings; Ziah decides in Linear. No infinite fix↔reject ping-pong, ever.

## Security persona (full file)

```markdown
---
name: security
stage: reviewer
maxTurns: 15
---
# Mission
You are the security reviewer on an autonomous coding crew. Judge ONLY the
diff for exploitable weaknesses. You cannot edit files. Assume the code will
face the public internet.

# Hunt for
- Injection: SQL/NoSQL built by string concat; shell exec with untrusted input; eval.
- AuthZ: endpoints/handlers missing auth checks; object access without ownership
  check (IDOR); privilege checks done client-side only.
- Secrets: keys, tokens, passwords hardcoded in the diff or written to logs.
- Dependencies: new/changed deps — typosquats, unmaintained packages, lockfile churn.
- SSRF/XSS: fetch of user-supplied URLs; unescaped user input in HTML/innerHTML;
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
- Fail-open: error paths that skip validation/auth; catch blocks that continue as
  authorized; disabled TLS/verification "temporarily".

# Ignore
- Style, naming, performance, test coverage, correctness bugs with no security
  impact — other reviewers own those. Pre-existing issues outside the diff.

# Output contract
List each finding on its own line: `SEVERITY(critical|high|medium|low): file:line — issue — fix`.
Reject ONLY for critical/high findings. If none, approve.
FINAL LINE must be exactly one of:
VERDICT: APPROVE
VERDICT: REJECT: <one-line reason>
```

## Cost math

Per bot-review round: 1 (correctness) + 0.5 (security avg) + 0.25 (design) + 0.3 (test-quality) ≈ **2.05 reviewer calls typical**. Worst single round = 4. Worst-case task (2 rounds, all triggers) = 8 reviewer calls + 2 worker calls — bounded by `reviewRounds ≤ 2`, and correctness short-circuit trims most failing rounds to 1 call. Typical task total: **2–3 reviewer calls**.

## Project overrides

Resolution order per persona name: `<repo>/.dgorch/personas/**` → orchestrator global `personas/**` → built-in default string (current prompts). Same filename shadows; a repo can also *add* reviewers by dropping `.dgorch/personas/reviewers/<custom>.md` with a `trigger:` frontmatter field (file globs, `userVisible`, or `always`) — the panel auto-includes any reviewer file whose trigger matches. Adding a persona = writing one markdown file in the repo; no config, no restart semantics beyond next task pickup.

---

# Annex C — Bulletproofing Fix Spec

## Fix spec

**P0-1 — Merges never touch the main repo (MUST)**
Fix: persistent integration worktree at `.dgorch/integration`, created in `ensureIntegrationBranch()` (`git worktree add .dgorch/integration <branch>`); `merge()` runs `git merge --no-ff` inside it. Delete the `checkout` at worktree.ts:56. Main repo HEAD is never read or moved.
Where: `src/worktree.ts` (`ensureIntegrationBranch`, `merge`).
Acceptance: after a full run, `git -C <repo> symbolic-ref HEAD` and `git status` are byte-identical to before; merges land on the integration branch; concurrent create/commit/merge never fails with "branch checked out elsewhere".
Test: chaos test — dirty main repo on a feature branch, run 4 parallel tasks; assert HEAD/branch/dirty files unchanged and integration branch contains all 4 merges.

**P0-2 — Rate-limit is a pause, not a failure (MUST)**
Fix: `runClaude` returns `errorKind: "rate_limit" | "auth" | "timeout" | "spawn" | "task"` classified from CLI JSON error text/stderr/exit (patterns: `usage limit`, `rate limit`, `429`, `overloaded`, `credit`, plus reset timestamp if present). `CliAgentRunner` breaks its edit-retry loop immediately on any infra kind. Conductor: infra kind → do NOT increment attempts; set run status `paused` with persisted `resumeAt` (runs table column); serve loop sleeps until `resumeAt` (exponential 1m→30m cap when no reset time given), then resumes via normal `execute()`.
Where: `src/claude-cli.ts`, `src/agent.ts`, `src/conductor.ts` (fail path), `src/store.ts` (+`resume_at`), `src/serve.ts`.
Acceptance: a simulated 429 mid-run consumes 0 attempts, run row shows `paused`+`resumeAt`, resume completes with zero dead-lettered tasks; kill-during-pause resumes correctly.
Test: FakeAgentRunner emits `errorKind:"rate_limit"` twice then succeeds; assert attempts stay 0, journal shows pause/resume, plan completes.

**P0-3 — Single-serve lock (MUST)**
Fix: `.dgorch/serve.lock` written `O_EXCL` with `{pid, startedAt}`; heartbeat mtime touch every 10s. On startup, if lock exists: `process.kill(pid,0)` alive → exit with clear error; dead or heartbeat >60s stale → steal. Release on clean exit. Also set `PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000` in Store.
Where: new `src/lock.ts`, called from `serveRun`/`startRun`/`resumeRun`; `src/store.ts`.
Acceptance: second `dgorch serve` on same repo exits ≠0 within 1s with actionable message; after `kill -9` of first, second acquires within one stale window.
Test: spawn two serve processes against one repo; assert exactly one runs; SIGKILL it; assert a third acquires.

**P1-a — Bounded human-rejection loop (MUST)**
Fix: new `human_bounces` column, incremented in `applyVerdicts` (never reset); at bounce 3 → `blocked` + Linear comment "needs manual intervention". Description: replace a delimited `<!-- dgorch:changes -->…` section instead of appending (history stays in journal). Inject only the newest 20 lessons into prompts.
Where: `src/verdicts.ts:applyVerdicts`, `src/store.ts`, `src/conductor.ts:toSpec`.
Acceptance: 3 consecutive rejections park the task blocked; description length stable across bounces; prompt lesson count capped.
Test: verdict test loops changes-requested ×4; assert final status `blocked`, one `dgorch:changes` section, ≤20 lessons in spec.

**P1-b — Scoped commits + un-truncatable review (MUST for secrets, SHOULD for diff budget)**
Fix: before `git add -A`, unstage/skip files matching a denylist (`.env*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `id_rsa*`, `*credentials*`) and files >1MB; log skips and surface them in the reviewer prompt. Reviewer: always send full `--stat`; if diff exceeds budget, send per-file diffs newest-first up to budget and force the verdict path to `human_review` (never auto-`done`) when truncated.
Where: `src/worktree.ts:commit`, `src/reviewer.ts` (`collectDiff`, `CliReviewer.review` return `truncated`), `src/conductor.ts`.
Acceptance: a worktree containing `.env` commits without it; truncated-diff tasks cannot reach `done` without a human.
Test: fake worker writes `.env`+code; assert commit excludes `.env`; oversized diff → task parks `human_review`.

**P1-c — Verdicts by state *type*; ignore bot comments (MUST)**
Fix: query Linear `state { name type }`; map `completed`→approve, `started|unstarted`→changes, `canceled`→new verdict "cancel" → task `dead` (journaled, Linear comment). Unknown → warn once per task. Prefix every bot-authored comment with sentinel `[dgorch]`; `newestComment` skips sentinel comments and any comment older than the task's `updatedAt` (parked time).
Where: `src/verdicts.ts` (query, `computeVerdicts`, `newestComment`), `src/linear.ts` (sentinel).
Acceptance: canceling an issue ends the task within one poll; a bot comment posted after parking is never returned as the human's change request.
Test: verdict tests for `canceled` type, custom state name with `started` type, and newest-comment = `[dgorch]` sentinel (must pick older human comment).

**P1-d — Global worktree/branch prune (SHOULD)**
Fix: at Conductor startup (after `recoverOrphans`), `git worktree list --porcelain` + `git branch --list 'orchestrator/task-*'`; remove any whose task isn't `in_progress` (none are at startup).
Where: `src/worktree.ts` (+`pruneAll`), `src/conductor.ts:execute`.
Acceptance: after N crash/resume cycles, `.dgorch/worktrees` and task branches contain only live entries.
Test: crash mid-task twice, resume, complete; assert zero leftover worktrees/branches.

**P2-a — Log/journal rotation (SHOULD)**
Fix: logger rotates jsonl at 50MB keep 3; `store.pruneJournal(runId, keepDays)` for terminal runs, invoked on serve start.
Acceptance: files bounded; journal for active runs untouched. Test: unit — write >cap, assert rotation.

**P2-b — Graceful kill + discard timeout edits (MUST)**
Fix: timeout sends SIGTERM, 10s grace, then SIGKILL (kill the process *group*: `detached:true`, `kill(-pid)`). Runner returns `errorKind:"timeout"`; Conductor then resets the worktree (`git checkout -- . && git clean -fd`) before the commit check, so half-written edits are never committed.
Where: `src/claude-cli.ts`, `src/conductor.ts:runTask`.
Acceptance: timed-out task leaves clean worktree; no timeout commit ever reaches integration.
Test: chaos — fake runner writes files then reports timeout; assert integration branch lacks them.

**P2-c — True cost accounting, atomic (SHOULD)**
Fix: parse `total_cost_usd` and full `usage` (input+output+cache) from CLI JSON; cap in USD. Thread a `costSink` into `CliPlanner`/`CliReviewer` so their calls count. Fold cost into `transition()` patch (one tx).
Acceptance: reported spend within 5% of summing CLI JSON across all calls; crash between result and transition can't lose/double cost.
Test: fake CLI JSON with usage on planner+reviewer+worker; assert run total = sum.

**P2-d — No dead run rows from planner failure (SHOULD)**
Fix: create run with status `planning`; planner throw → `plan_failed` (journaled). `resume`/`serve` on a task-less run re-plans idempotently.
Acceptance: failed planning is resumable with the same runId; no orphan `active` rows.
Test: planner throws on run 1, succeeds on resume; assert same runId completes.

**Files-overlap guard vs empty `files:[]` (MUST)**
Fix: in `planToTasks`, validate: duplicate keys, unknown deps, cycles. In `pickReady`, empty `files` = conflicts-with-everything (runs alone); planner prompt requires non-empty files, retry-on-parse already exists.
Acceptance: a plan mixing empty and non-empty files never runs the empty-files task concurrently with anything.
Test: chaos — plan with `files:[]` task + 3 others, concurrency 4; assert it executes solo.

## New chaos tests
1. Dirty-main-repo parallel merge (P0-1). 2. Rate-limit pause/kill-during-pause/resume (P0-2). 3. Dual-serve lock + SIGKILL takeover (P0-3). 4. 4× human rejection → blocked, bounded description (P1-a). 5. Secret file never committed; truncated diff never auto-done (P1-b). 6. Canceled issue + bot-comment poisoning (P1-c). 7. Repeated crash → zero leaked worktrees/branches (P1-d). 8. Timeout edits never merge (P2-b). 9. Empty-files task runs solo (files-guard). 10. SIGTERM to serve mid-task → children dead, resume clean.

## Anything auditors missed
- **Orphaned claude children (MUST):** conductor crash/SIGTERM leaves `claude -p` processes running and editing worktrees being deleted on resume. Track child pids; process-group kill on exit signals; `recoverOrphans` must also kill any survivor before recreating worktrees.
- **Linear drift from fire-and-forget `void linear.setStatus` (SHOULD):** failed updates are silently dropped forever; add a reconcile sweep each serve cycle (store status vs Linear state, re-push diffs).
- **Preflight `dgorch doctor` (SHOULD):** verify claude CLI on PATH + authenticated (`claude -p ping`), git ≥2.30, Linear key valid, disk space, repo not mid-rebase — fail fast with one-line remedies.
- **State backup (SHOULD):** `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` + copy `state.sqlite` to `.dgorch/backups/` at run start, keep 5.
- **bypassPermissions blast radius (SHOULD, document):** workers can run arbitrary shell; minimum: `--disallowedTools` for network/system-destructive Bash patterns, or macOS `sandbox-exec` wrapper as fast follow.

Key files: `/Users/ziah/Coding projects/DeepGem-Config/orchestrator-v2/src/{worktree,claude-cli,conductor,verdicts,store,agent,reviewer,serve,bootstrap,planner,logger}.ts`, tests in `/Users/ziah/Coding projects/DeepGem-Config/orchestrator-v2/test/chaos.test.ts`.

---

# Annex D — Critique Resolutions (rev 2)

*Two adversarial reviewers (simplicity, buildability) audited rev 1: 26 issues, 4 blockers. Every issue below is folded into the PRD rev 2; where an annex above conflicts with this section, THIS SECTION WINS.*

**#1 [blocker] PRD Pillar B / UX step 3 ("question asked ... tap A. Build continues.") + Annex A questions table; vs orchestr**
- Issue: M2's exit criterion depends on mid-task questions, but the engine has no way to ask one. Workers are single-shot `claude -p` invocations — they cannot pause, emit a question, and continue. The annex specs the answer side (SQLite questions table, reply routing, first-wins) but nev
- Resolution: RESOLVED — mid-task questions kept, specced as blocker #16's mechanism (worker QUESTION output line → blocked → answer → ready). Rides existing machinery.

**#2 [major] PRD Pillar B noise control + Annex A (30-min human_review digest, quiet hours 22:00–08:00 with morning digest,**
- Issue: Three stacked rate-limiting systems for a single-user bot whose realistic volume is ~5–15 messages per run. The digest and the quiet-hours 'morning digest' require holding pending notifications with flush scheduling — which contradicts the annex's own claim that 'undelivered noti
- Resolution: RESOLVED — digest/quiet-hours/hourly-cap CUT. All notifications immediate; NOTIFY_QUIET_HOURS and NOTIFY_MAX_PER_HOUR deleted.

**#3 [major] PRD Pillar C workers + Annex B worker routing (kind enum on PlannedTask, 5 worker persona files, planner promp**
- Issue: Worker personas have no success metric (PRD §8 measures bulletproof, pocket, security catch rate, setup — nothing measures worker-persona value) and the PRD itself concedes the value is marginal: 'mis-tags degrade gracefully (every persona is a competent coder with different emph
- Resolution: RESOLVED — worker personas + kind enum DEFERRED to Phase 3. M3 = reviewer personas only. Security trigger clause (d) dropped.

**#4 [major] PRD §4 Pillar A table (headed 'All MUSTs before anything else ships') + §7 M1/M4; vs Annex C severity labels**
- Issue: The Pillar A table conflates MUSTs and SHOULDs, so M1's scope is ambiguous. Annex C marks P1-d (worktree prune), P2-a (log rotation), P2-c (USD cost), P2-d (resumable planning), and the diff-budget half of P1-b as SHOULD — yet all appear in the 'All MUSTs' table, and M4 then re-l
- Resolution: RESOLVED — Pillar A table now carries a Milestone column; M1 = P0-1..3, P1-a, P1-b(secrets+diff-budget), P1-c(+moved-guard), P2-b, orphaned-children, files-guard, prune. M4 = P2-a, P2-c, P2-d, doctor, backups. Reconcile sweep = M2. No item in two milestones.

**#5 [major] Annex B review panel — design-fidelity reviewer (trigger: task.userVisible === true); vs orchestrator-v2/src/c**
- Issue: Design-fidelity fires exclusively on userVisible tasks — the exact set the engine already parks at human_review for Ziah personally (conductor.ts:213: `task.userVisible ? "human_review" : "done"`). The PRD's own justification for the security heuristic ('human review still backst
- Resolution: RESOLVED — design-fidelity reviewer CUT from v1 panel (userVisible already human-gated). Panel = correctness + security + test-quality.

**#6 [major] Annex A two-way flow: free-text grammar (`ok 12`, `fix 12 <note>`, bare `1`/`A`/`yes` inference, `Q7 A` disamb**
- Issue: The PRD's core argument for Telegram over SMS is that 'inline buttons give one-tap answers (better than parsing A from a text)' — and then the annex specs a full free-text parsing grammar anyway: bare-letter inference against the single open question, a re-prompt flow for ambigui
- Resolution: RESOLVED — buttons for every decision; free text only for the fix-note after 'Request changes'. Bare-letter grammar, ok/fix command language, and disambiguation flows deleted.

**#7 [major] Annex A config: 'Chat ID auto-captured: user sends /start once; bot stores chat_id in SQLite. That's the entir**
- Issue: Anyone who discovers the bot's handle can send /start; nothing authenticates that the captured chat_id is Ziah. Since replies drive approvals (`ok 12` merges code toward client repos) and plan-gate GO spends money, an unauthenticated first-/start-wins registration is a real hole 
- Resolution: RESOLVED — pairing required: serve prints one-time code; bot binds only on /start <code>; all other chat ids ignored.

**#8 [major] Annex B project overrides: repo-local custom reviewers auto-included via `trigger:` frontmatter (file globs / **
- Issue: This is a plugin architecture — frontmatter trigger DSL, glob matching, panel auto-discovery — shipped for a user base of one with zero custom reviewers in existence. Same-name shadowing (edit the security prompt per-repo) delivers nearly all the practical value at a fraction of 
- Resolution: RESOLVED — trigger DSL / auto-discovery CUT. Same-filename shadowing only; adding a reviewer = file + one line in the hardcoded panel list.

**#9 [minor] PRD Pillar B plan-approval gate + Annex A `plan_ready` event (mechanics unspecced)**
- Issue: The gate is the right call — keep it — but neither doc specs how the engine waits: it implies a new run state and a bespoke GO path ('waits for GO (button tap or Linear)'), and the message promises 'est $4' when nothing in the system estimates cost (P2-c only accounts actuals). T
- Resolution: RESOLVED — plan gate = question #0 in the questions table; message shows task count + cost cap (facts), no invented estimate.

**#10 [minor] Annex B anti-loop: new `reviewRounds` column, max 2; vs orchestrator-v2/src/conductor.ts:210,223-234**
- Issue: Bot-review rejections already flow through `fail()` and are bounded by the existing `maxAttempts` counter — the new column is a third bounded counter (attempts, reviewRounds, human_bounces) where two would do. The only behavioral delta is terminal state: `blocked` (for Ziah) inst
- Resolution: RESOLVED (merged with #19) — no reviewRounds column. Review rejections consume the existing attempts counter; exhaustion via review-rejection terminates as blocked (not dead) via a reason check in fail().

**#11 [minor] Annex A NotifyEvent: `cost_threshold` event and `run_complete.stats.costTokens`**
- Issue: cost_threshold has no defined threshold anywhere (not in the config vars, not in RunConfig), and `costTokens` in run_complete contradicts P2-c/M1 moving cost to USD before M2 ships — a stale field in the interface spec.
- Resolution: RESOLVED — cost_threshold event cut; notify on the existing cost_cap stop. Stats field renamed costUsd.

**#12 [minor] Annex A config: `NOTIFY_CHANNEL=telegram|none`**
- Issue: A config var that duplicates information the system already has — TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN present means telegram, absent means none. Every avoidable var works against the '3-item setup' success metric.
- Resolution: RESOLVED — NOTIFY_CHANNEL deleted; channel inferred from TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN presence, logged at serve start.

**#13 [minor] PRD Pillar C / Annex B: performance reviewer 'off by default — enable per-project' (0% of tasks)**
- Issue: A shipped-but-disabled feature is a hedge, not a decision: it adds a persona file and an enablement path that run on nothing. The persona system's whole pitch is that adding a reviewer later is writing one markdown file.
- Resolution: RESOLVED — performance reviewer deleted entirely from Phase 2 (listed in non-goals).

**#14 [minor] PRD §7 milestone order: Linear reconcile sweep in M4; vs orchestrator-v2/src/conductor.ts:140,163,208,215 (`vo**
- Issue: Every Linear status push is fire-and-forget and silently droppable today. That's tolerable while Ziah sits at a desk with the terminal open, but M2 makes him remote-only with Linear as his sole view — silent drift then means the board he approves from lies to him. The fix for dri
- Resolution: RESOLVED — Linear reconcile sweep moved from M4 to M2 (store-vs-Linear diff re-push each serve cycle).

**#15 [blocker] PRD Pillar B 'plan-approval gate' + Decision 2; annexes have no corresponding spec (Annex A only defines the p**
- Issue: The plan gate — a default-on behavior change — has no mechanism anywhere: no run state name for 'waiting on GO' (Run.status today is active|awaiting_human|complete|failed, types.ts:58), no statement of where the engine waits (bootstrap.ts createRunWithPlan? a new Conductor StopRe
- Resolution: RESOLVED (blocker) — plan-gate mechanism specced: run status awaiting_plan_approval entered after insertTasks and before first execute(); serve loop owns the wait, polling the questions table AND the Linear plan issue (completed-type move = GO, canceled-type = abort); GO callback carries plan:<runId>:go; --no-plan-gate skips; no-channel runs still gate via Linear (or --no-plan-gate).

**#16 [blocker] Annex A 'Two-way flow' (questions table) + PRD Pillar B / UX step 3**
- Issue: The entire question feature has no producer side. Workers are one-shot 'claude -p' invocations (CliAgentRunner, agent.ts:56) with no way to ask anything; the annex specs the questions table, buttons, and answer routing, but never: how a question is created (by whom, detected how 
- Resolution: RESOLVED (blocker) — question PRODUCER specced: worker final-line output contract 'QUESTION: <prompt> | A) x B) y | REC: <letter>' parsed from the CLI result; task → blocked (question_open journaled, zero attempts consumed, worktree removed); question row inserted; notify fired. Answer → description gains 'ANSWER: …' → blocked→ready (guarded). First-wins via store.transition gaining optional expectedStatus compare-and-set; loser is a logged no-op.

**#17 [blocker] Annex C P1-c ('map completed→approve, started|unstarted→changes') vs STATUS_TO_LINEAR in src/linear.ts:42-51**
- Issue: The type-based mapping self-triggers on the states the engine itself parks tasks in. human_review mirrors to 'Human Review' and blocked mirrors to 'In Progress' — custom Linear states must carry a standard type, and both of these will be type 'started'. As written, the first poll
- Resolution: RESOLVED (blocker) — verdict requires MOVEMENT: persist the parked Linear state id on the task; poll compares current state id to parked id and skips when equal; canceled-type → dead; blocked keeps 'In Progress' mirror safely because only a human MOVE triggers a verdict. Kills the started-type self-recycle loop.

**#18 [major] Annex B 'Routing back' — '…into the existing fail() path — the retry worker prompt gets them via the lessons m**
- Issue: False about the current code, and it silently guts M3's feedback loop. fail() (conductor.ts:223) writes the reason only to task.error; lessons come exclusively from store.listLessons, which is populated only by applyVerdicts on human change-requests (verdicts.ts:153). TaskSpec ha
- Resolution: RESOLVED — review-rejection reasons wired to the retry: on panel reject, store.addLesson(runId,'[<reviewer>] <reason>',taskId) before fail(), AND TaskSpec gains previousRejection rendered in buildWorkerPrompt. Acceptance: retry prompt contains the reviewer text verbatim.

**#19 [major] Annex B 'Anti-loop' (reviewRounds ≤ 2 → blocked) vs the existing fail() path (conductor.ts:223, maxAttempts=3 **
- Issue: Two counters now govern the same rejection event and the spec never says how they interact. Panel rejections route 'into the existing fail() path', which increments attempts and dead-letters at maxAttempts — so does a review rejection consume an attempt AND a reviewRound? With ma
- Resolution: RESOLVED — see #10. One counter; interaction unambiguous; gate failures unchanged (attempts→dead).

**#20 [major] Annex C P2-c (cost in USD) vs types.ts:59-60/67 (costSpent/costCap in output tokens), index.ts:20/65 ('--cost-**
- Issue: The tokens→USD re-denomination has no migration or compatibility story: existing state.sqlite runs store cost_spent as token counts (e.g. 150000), so resuming an old run under USD semantics instantly trips any sane USD cap and halts with cost_cap; the CLI flag keeps the same name
- Resolution: RESOLVED — additive cost_usd column; cap compares USD only; legacy rows logged and start at 0; --cost-cap-usd with deprecated --cost-cap alias; NotifyEvent uses costUsd.

**#21 [major] Annex A 'Failure behavior'/'Risks' vs serveLoop (serve.ts:56-93): how the Telegram getUpdates loop coexists wi**
- Issue: serveLoop is strictly sequential — Conductor.execute() blocks until the run parks, and verdicts are applied only between cycles. Phone taps (plan GO, question answers, 'ok 12' replies) must land while execute() is in flight, but the spec never says the replies loop is a concurren
- Resolution: RESOLVED — process model specced: single process; Telegram long-poll runs as a concurrent promise sharing the synchronous store; inbound taps write to SQLite then poke the serve wait (same poke as webhooks); offset persisted for resume; 'lock in SQLite' risk note replaced by the serve.lock which already guarantees single instance.

**#22 [minor] Annex A message formats: 'ok 12' / 'fix 12 <note>' / 'Review "Checkout flow" (12/18)'**
- Issue: '12' is undefined. Engine task ids are UUIDs (planner.ts:27 randomUUID) and the store persists only the opaque linearIssueId — not the human-readable Linear identifier (DGI-12) or issue number. '(12/18)' reads as 'task 12 of 18', which collides with an id-like reply token. The re
- Resolution: RESOLVED — the human-readable Linear identifier (e.g. DEE-12) is persisted on the task row at upsert and used in all outbound cards and callback_data. No short-id mapping.

**#23 [minor] Annex B 'Worker routing' — 'Add one enum to PlannedTask in src/planner.ts'**
- Issue: kind is consumed long after planning — the conductor resolves personas/workers/<kind>.md at task pickup and the security trigger (d) reads task.kind at review time — so it must live on Task, the tasks table (additive migration like linear_issue_id, store.ts:74-77), planToTasks ma
- Resolution: MOOT — kind enum deferred with worker personas (#3).

**#24 [minor] Annex B review panel — design-fidelity 'prompt includes design-ref paths from run config'**
- Issue: RunConfig (types.ts:64-77) has no design-ref field; its name, shape, and how it's populated (CLI flag? .dgorch file?) are unspecified, so the design-fidelity reviewer can't be built as written.
- Resolution: MOOT — design-fidelity cut (#5); designRefs config need disappears.

**#25 [minor] Annex A 'Facts' + PRD Pillar B: 'two-way replies require a public webhook URL'**
- Issue: Overstated as a researched fact: Twilio inbound SMS can also be retrieved by polling the Messages REST API (no public URL), which is exactly the long-poll shape the Telegram design uses — impractical (latency, no push) but possible. The Telegram-first decision still stands comfor
- Resolution: RESOLVED — SMS fact softened: two-way SMS is webhook-first (API polling possible but poor); decision weight rests on A2P lead time + cost.

**#26 [minor] Annex C P0-2 pause/resume vs Conductor StopReason (conductor.ts:32) and dgorch run (index.ts:12-39)**
- Issue: Pause requires Conductor.execute() to return, but StopReason is closed ('complete'|'awaiting_human'|'cost_cap') and no new value is named; ServeOutcome likewise. And non-serve 'dgorch run' behavior on pause is unspecified — exit non-zero? sleep in-process until resumeAt? The one-
- Resolution: RESOLVED — StopReason gains 'paused' (LoopOutcome carries resumeAt); serve sleeps and re-executes; dgorch run prints 'paused until <t>' and exits 0.
